steps.title: Find the slope of line k using two points
steps.content:
From the graph, line k passes through two clear points: $(-6, 0)$ and $(0, -5)$. Using the slope formula:
$$m_k = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{-5 - 0}{0 - (-6)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$
So line k has slope $-\frac{5}{6}$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular slope relationship
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals of each other. If line k has slope $m_k$, then line j has slope $m_j = -\frac{1}{m_k}$.
$$m_j = -\frac{1}{-\frac{5}{6}} = \frac{6}{5} = 1.2$$
The slope of line j is 1.2.
